The proposal is to permit a communication stair opening between 19th and 20th floors at this fully- sprinklered office building. Understanding that there is no fundamental difference between the proposed communication stair and the permitted escalator or moving walk, the alternative solution is only required because the floor assembly opening is 11.05m2, which is 1.05m2 larger than the permitted 10m2 opening. OBC 3.2.8.2(5) states:Except as permitted by Sentence (6), openings for escalators and inclined moving walks need not conform to therequirements in Articles 3.2.8.3. to 3.2.8.8. provided(a) the opening for each escalator or walk does not exceed 10 m2,(b) the building is sprinklered throughout,(c) closely spaced sprinklers and associated draft stops are installed around the openings in conformance with NFPA 13, ¿Standard for the Installation of Sprinkler Systems,¿ and (d) the interconnected floor space contains only Group A, Division 1, 2 or 3, Group D or Group E occupancies. To compensate for the larger floor assembly opening, the proposed alternative solution is to contain the area above the opening with a zero-rated fire separation, and locating a smoke detector, and two sprinklers inside this contained area. The proposed alternative solution meets the Objective and Functional Statements by limiting and reducing the potential for impact of fire beyond its point of origin, through the containment of heat and smoke on the 20th floor, which will also expedite activation of the fire alarm system.

The purpose of the Zoning By-law amendment applications is to permit 4 residential and mixed use buildings with heights of 43, 31, 29, and 25 storeys. The applicant is proposing a total of 1,542 residential units and 1,902 parking spaces.  The applicant is proposing the development be built in 2 phases with phase 1 consisting of the 43 and 31 storey buildings and phase 2 consisting of the 29 and 25 storey buildings. The properties subject to the zoning by-law amendment application are 5945-5949 Yonge Street, 5959 Yonge Street, 1 & 2 Doverwood Court, 15 Wedgewood Drive, and 48 Cummer Avenue. This site is also subject to related Official Plan Amendment application (file # 16 156153 NNY 24 OZ) which applies to all of the following properties 5925-5931,  5935A, 5945-5949, 5959, and 5995 Yonge Street, 11, 15, 17, 19, 21, 23, and 25 Wedgewood Drive, 1-8 Doverwood Court, and 42, 44, 46, 48, 50, 52, and 54 Cummer Avenue.

Proposed Official Plan Amendment and Zoning By-law Amendment applications contemplate the redevelopment of the Subject Site with two towers having heights of 80 storeys and 85 storeys (respectively 279.6 metres and 296 metres, inclusive of mechanical and architectural features), anchored by a 3- to 14-storey podium element. The Proposed Development will contain 2,406 dwelling units, including 109 affordable rental dwelling units, 237 hotel suites and an associated restaurant and bar, 1,816 square metres of at-grade retail space, a 955 square metre childcare centre and a 1,716 square metre community space. A total gross floor area of 220,333 square metres is proposed, resulting in an overall density of 19.6 times the area of the Subject Site (Parcel B). The existing 25-storey office building on the Subject Site is proposed to be demolished to accommodate the redevelopment.

The current proposal seeks to add an additional 10 storeys to each tower resulting in a total height of 48 and 55 storeys (165.3 metres and 186.3 metres to the top of the mechanical penthouses), inclusive of the previously approved 5 and 7-storey base building. The proposal includes a total GFA of 78,725 square metres, including 5,783 square metres of retail GFA, and a density of 13.71 FSI.

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes the development of a 13-storey seniors residence providing residential suites, assisted living suites and memory care suites. The building will provide a total of 251 residential units, being a mix of studios, 1-bedroom, 1-bedroom + den and 2-bedroom units.  A paved pick up/drop off forecourt along the western property line will be provided, leading to 1 level of below-grade parking, providing a total of 24 parking spaces; as well as 35 bicycle parking spaces. The proposal also includes a 205 square metre public park addition to Snider Parkette and 370 square metre privately owned publicly accessible spaces (POPS).

Official Plan Amendment and Zoning By-law Amendment application for three towers of 65, 75 and 96 storeys proposed above the existing designated heritage College Park building, which will form the base of the towers. A total of 2,339 residential units are proposed, including the replacement of 210 existing rental units, 6 additional rental replacement units, and 2,123 additional dwelling units. The proposal also includes 5,527 square metres of cultural space, a 1,488 square metre daycare facility, a 19,373 square metres of hotel space, 43,000 square metres of commercial and retail space and 600 square metres of new on-site parkland to expand the existing adjacent College Park.

The Local Planning Appeal Tribunal (LPAT) decision of July 20, 2018 approved in principle the redevelopment of the site (5840-5870 Yonge St) for mixed-use development.  Refer to Case Number PL160863.  This site plan application proposes a 32-storey residential building containing 408 dwelling units and three levels of underground parking.  The total gross floor area proposed is 33,205 square metres.  A park of 734 square metres is also proposed at the south-east corner of the site.  A future site plan application will be filed for 5870 Yonge Street for an automotive dealership with 3,763 square metres of gross floor area.  The residential building will also be provided vehicular access from the driveway serving the automotive dealership with mutual access easements from an associated consent application.

The building will provide a total of 699 residential units, being a mix of 1-bedroom, 1-bedroom+den, 2-bedroom, and 3-bedroom units. The building will also provide retail units at grade. The Site Plan Control Application proposes the development of three retail commercial buildings with a total gross floor area of 4,071 square metres and a floor space index of 0.36. Access will be provided from York Mills Road leading to a surface parking area with 111 vehicle spaces and 33 bicycle parking spaces. Building A, a one-storey structure, is designed to accommodate either four mid-sized commercial units or a single larger tenant such as a grocery store. Building B is a two-storey structure intended to provide up to seven smaller commercial units on the ground floor with a daycare on the second floor. Building C, a one-storey structure, is designed to accommodate a single tenant, likely a restaurant.

Site Plan Amendment related to file 20 115847 NNY 08 SA proposes to consolidate many of the previously proposed bachelor and one-bedroom units to create more functional two- and three-bedroom layouts. As a result, the overall unit count has been reduced from 704 to 500 units, with two-bedroom units now comprising 63% of the total unit mix and three-bedroom units accounting for 13%. To accommodate these larger unit configurations, localized increases in height have been introduced, driven by architectural design considerations, structural and mechanical coordination requirements, and mandatory code compliance measures necessary to support the revised layouts.

Official Plan Amendment to propose maintaining the existing 12-storey office building located in the general centre of the 235 Yorkland property. The proposal accommodates both a north building (Building B) and a south building (Building A) with a private road/driveway access via a driveway to/from Yorkland Boulevard. The existing surface parking is proposed to be relocated below grade with the exception of a limited supply of parking spaces located at grade abutting the existing office building for visitor and convenience parking purposes. The proposal contemplates a new public park in the northeast corner of the site along Yorkland Boulevard, providing a new green space and community amenity for the entire area.

A total of 193 vehicle parking spaces are proposed in 4 levels of underground parking, and 658 bicycle parking spaces are proposed.

Site Plan Amendment application to further amend the previously approved Site Plan Amendment application for the site (File No. 18 203283 STE 27 SA) to permit a raised terrace and canopy at the southwest corner of the existing building along the southern portion of the west (Bay Street) façade and along a portion of the south (Yorkville Avenue) façade. This application would modify the previous approval to reduce the northern extent of the canopy and eliminate a ramp along Bay Street and expand the eastern extent of the terrace under the approved canopy along Yorkville Avenue.

Official Plan Amendment to propose maintaining the existing 12-storey office building located in the general centre of the 235 Yorkland property. The proposal accommodates both a north building (Building B) and a south building (Building A) with a private road/driveway access via a driveway to/from Yorkland Boulevard. The existing surface parking is proposed to be relocated below grade with the exception of a limited supply of parking spaces located at grade abutting the existing office building for visitor and convenience parking purposes. The proposal contemplates a new public park in the northeast corner of the site along Yorkland Boulevard, providing a new green space and community amenity for the entire area.

Site Plan Control Application to permit the construction of an 14-storey mixed used building with a total of 77 proposed residential units and proposed ground level commercial space of 400 square metres. The proposed gross floor area of the building would be 14,662 square metres with a proposed FSI of 7.95. Parking would be provided in a 3-level underground parking structure containing 106 parking spaces.The Zoning By-law Amendment application was appealed to the Ontario Land Tribunal (Case number OLT-21-001055) and all parties agreed to a settlement in the spring of 2022 (April/May). A Settlement Report went to City Council on July 19, 2022 and Zoning By-law 1248-2022  was the outcome of the settlement discussions. A Section 37/Section 111 Agreement was registered in December 2022.

The Local Planning Appeal Tribunal (LPAT) decision of July 20, 2018 approved in principle the redevelopment of the site for a residential condominium building and an automotive dealership.  Refer to Case Number PL160863.  This site plan application for 5868 Yonge Street proposes an automotive dealership with 3,763 square metres of gross floor area.  The residential condo building will also be provided vehicular access from the driveway serving the automotive dealership with mutual access easements from an associated consent application. This site plan application proposes a 32-storey residential building containing 408 dwelling units and three levels of underground parking (Refer to: 19 108243 NNY SA).

Site Plan Control Application to permit the construction of an 14-storey mixed used building with a total of 77 proposed residential units and proposed ground level commercial space of 400 square metres. The proposed gross floor area of the building would be 14,662 square metres with a proposed FSI of 7.95. Parking would be provided in a 3-level underground parking structure containing 106 parking spaces.The Zoning By-law Amendment application was appealed to the Ontario Land Tribunal (Case number OLT-21-001055) and all parties agreed to a settlement in the spring of 2022 (April/May). A Settlement Report went to City Council on July 19, 2022 and Zoning By-law 1248-2022  was the outcome of the settlement discussions. A Section 37/Section 111 Agreement was registered in December 2022.

A Development Application has been submitted to amend the Official Plan and Zoning By-law to allow for a long-term, mixed use plan for the entire Yorkdale Shopping Centre site. The original application included three conceptual block master plan options that comprised of a range of retail, office, hotel and residential uses with a maximum proposed total future gross floor area of 682,670 square metres and 2.29 Floor Space Index and an internal private above and below grade street network. The application has been revised into a single concept with expanded parkland and the inclusion of a public road. The proposal continues to propose a range of uses, including affordable housing which is intended to occur in a phased manner over the next 20+ years

Site Plan Application for the second phase of the Newtonbrook Plaza development.  Block 2 proposes a total gross floor area of 66,838.9 square metres, consisting of 63,152.3 square metres of residential GFA and 3,628.0 square metres of commercial GFA.  The proposed mixed use building would contain two towers on a shared podium. One tower will be 34 storeys (106.3 metres excluding the mechanical penthouse) and the other proposed tower would be 40 storeys (123.5 metres excluding the mechanical penthouse). A total of 856 residential units are proposed with 543 vehicular parking spaces.  A new community centre and child care centre are also proposed as part of Phase 2.

Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment to permit a 25-storey mixed-use building, containing 224 dwelling units and 2,542 square metres of non-residential gross floor area. The new development is located at the northeast corner of the existing Atrium on Bay and would involve demolition of the existing 4 to 8-storey retail and office building at that location. The remainder of the Atrium on Bay would be retained and the new development will be integrated with the existing building at the ground floor, level 2, and concourse level.

The application proposes one new tower 27-storeys in height, with a total of 305 residential units. The tower is located in the western portion of a site that is already developed with an existing shopping plaza and surface parking lot. The existing private driveway to the shopping plaza will also provide access to the tower and its associated surface parking and pick-up and drop-off areas from York Gate Boulevard in the west. A new POPS is proposed south of the tower that will connect to future public realm elements envisioned for the block through the Jane Finch Secondary Plan.
